description: Adds or updates a logs panel that binds to a configured workbench log tool (CanvasBlock with type logs and ToolGraph content). The UI will run the named tool with the given arguments to fetch log lines or aggregates. Use `summary` to tell the operator what they should see; use `tool_name` that exists on this workbench (same string as the tool's configured name). `layout` is required for grid position and size.

identifier.description: Unique key for this canvas block (e.g. `loki_errors`, `datadog_apache`).

layout.description: Required grid placement (CanvasBlock.Layout). All of x, y, w, and h must be set.

x.description: Column index of the top-left cell (0-based).

y.description: Row index of the top-left cell (0-based).

props.description: Log widget configuration (WorkbenchJobResult.ToolGraph): human-readable framing plus the tool invocation.

title.description: Panel title shown above the log viewer (e.g. `Recent 5xx from checkout`).

summary.description: Optional one- or two-sentence explanation of what this query shows and why it matters for the investigation.

query.description: Which workbench tool to call and with what arguments (WorkbenchJobResult.ToolQuery).

tool_name.description: Name of the workbench tool to execute.  This is likely reported in prior observability subagent results.

tool_args.description: JSON string passed verbatim to that tool's input schema (e.g. query string, time range, filters). Keys and shape must match the tool's documented parameters.

summary.description: Optional short note stored with the query metadata (can duplicate or extend `props.summary`).
